title: 78 Years Since the Nakba: Ongoing Displacement, Denied Return, and Enduring Resistance
content:
Today we mark the Nakba, the ‘catastrophe’, and remember the more than 750,000 Palestinians forcibly expelled from their homeland in 1948, some 530 villages destroyed and the families forced to live in an exile that has never ended. For many families, their only ties to home and hope of return are now their house keys, which they hold on to.

Seventy-eight years later, almost six million Palestinians remain registered as refugees. The Nakba was never a single date in history. It is a continuing reality, sustained through occupation, siege and the denial of return.

title: Mexico:The recent closure of the federal cases against human rights defender Kenia Hernández opens an opportunity to deliver justice and secure her release
content:
The undersigned organisations call for a solution for the imprisonment of Kenia Inés Hernández Montalván, an amuzga indigenous woman and human rights defender who has been detained since October 2020.

The human rights defender has faced 10 criminal cases against her since 2010

title: CIVICUS at the 87th Ordinary Session of the African Commission on Human and Peoples' Rights
content:
The 87th session of the African Commission on Human and Peoples’ Rights (ACHPR) will be held in Banjul, The Gambia, from 11 to 20 May. The session will be preceded by the NGO Forum, also in Banjul, from 7 to 9 May.

The Forum on the Participation of NGOs provides an opportunity for NGOs to share updates on the human rights situation on the continent. The themes that will be covered at this Forum are derived from the African Union’s (AU) theme of the year: 'Assuring Sustainable Water Availability and Safe Sanitation Systems to Achieve the Goals of Agenda 2063'.
